results.name: Kuwait
results.key_finding: ALREADY HAS A BASELINE (25.0, rank 121, Developing) — not unassessed despite the scan note. Iran's Jun 28 missile/drone attack on Ali Al Salem base is IRAN's conduct (attribution), not Kuwait's. Kuwait's OWN conduct — effective interception, no fatalities, two minor shrapnel injuries, no internal crackdown — is consistent with its Developing band. Surfaced on a negative-theater event: directionality blocks an upgrade; no new own-harm blocks a downgrade. Stale cache 20.0/rank126 refreshed to live 25.0/rank121. Confirm. Watch EQU (bidoon/migrant emergency protection).

results.name: Bahrain
results.key_finding: ALREADY HAS A BASELINE (9.4, rank 166, Critical) — not unassessed. Iran's Jun 28 drone damaged a Muharraq home (no casualties); the harm is IRAN's conduct (Amnesty's war-crimes research targets Iran), not Bahrain's. Bahrain's 9.4 already prices its OWN documented repression (2011-uprising legacy, political prisoners, citizenship-stripping, Shia-majority discrimination). No new within-window own-conduct either direction. Stale cache 4.4/rank170 refreshed to live 9.4/rank166. Confirm.

results.name: Nigeria
results.key_finding: 35M IPC Phase 3+ / 15,000 Phase 5 Borno already priced in the Jun 19 band crossing to 18.0. Double-count screened. WFP imminent-cuts and a formal Phase 5 declaration are forward triggers. No materially new development Jun 29.

results.name: Lebanon
results.key_finding: Jun 28 Washington framework collapsed within 48 hours (Israel resumed strikes, Hezbollah rejected it). The forward-trigger positive (implementation) did not materialize — no upgrade. Renewed war harm is Israel's conduct (attribution). No new Lebanese own-conduct either direction.

results.name: Venezuela
results.key_finding: Day-5 earthquake response (1,450 dead). The quake is a natural disaster (not own-conduct). Government response stays genuinely mixed — broad aid accepted (UNICEF shipments, US OFAC GL-60, $150M) vs slow/opaque relief — netting sub-threshold both directions. Confirm 18.0; watch ACT + ACC.

results.name: Qatar
results.key_finding: ALREADY HAS A BASELINE (10.9, rank 163). Al Udeid strike and a Qatari shrapnel death are IRAN's conduct (attribution). Qatar's Doha-mediation role (Jun 30 talks) is mild positive own-conduct but consistent with its established posture — sub-threshold. 10.9 prices chronic migrant-labor/rights limits. Stale cache 5.9/rank165 refreshed to live 10.9/rank163. Confirm.

results.name: UnitedHealth Group
results.key_finding: DOJ criminal/civil/antitrust probes are investigations, not convictions (pre-adjudication). Jun 23 DOJ takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) is sector context, not entity-named. Near-floor 10.2 already prices the alleged pattern. No new UHG-specific development Jun 29.

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: UN COI genocide finding (Jun 23) and resumed Lebanon strikes (Jun 28) are negative own-conduct. The framework did not mandate withdrawal — not positive own-conduct. No lower score exists.

results.name: Burkina Faso
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced: 52.8M Sahel food insecurity + second-largest IDP population continue the already-priced (Jun 1 reweighted) arc. Sahel calibration flag-for-review remains open at coordinator level (Mali 12.5 vs BF 6.3); no assessor one-off.

results.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced: third-largest-ever Ebola outbreak (1,155 cases/304 deaths, Jun 25; own-conduct response weakness) + M23 mass-killing harm attributed to Rwanda. Compounding already-priced crises; no new threshold.

results.name: Saudi Arabia
results.key_finding: Iranian strikes since Feb (38+ missiles/435+ drones) and Amnesty's war-crimes research are IRAN's conduct (attribution), not Saudi's. Saudi's 9.4 already prices its OWN record (executions, dissent suppression). No new score-moving own-conduct in-window. Confirm.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: Near-floor reinforced. The Gulf strikes ARE Iran's own negative conduct (correctly scored against Iran, unlike the Gulf states); 784+ executions YTD (37-year high), hunger strikes Week 126+. Continuation of an already-priced pattern; military-base strikes with limited civilian casualties do not clear a new threshold toward the floor.

results.name: Yemen
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: Houthi authorities forced WFP to terminate all 365 northern staff; 73 UN staff detained; 22.3M need aid; 12.7% appeal funded. Governing-actor own conduct. No lower score exists.

results.name: Sudan
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: al-Burhan rejected the Quad humanitarian ceasefire Jun 29; El Obeid 500,000+ besieged, UN window 'rapidly narrowing'; famine in El-Fasher/Kadugli. RSF's ceasefire acceptance is unimplemented (forward trigger). No lower score exists.

results.name: South Sudan
results.key_finding: Floor reinforced: 7.8M food insecure (56%), 2.2M children acutely malnourished, four counties at famine risk, 140,000 displaced in Akobo. Already-priced; no lower score exists. No major new development Jun 29.

kuwait_bahrain_determination: BOTH ALREADY IN THE INDEX WITH BASELINES — no new baseline needed to be established. Kuwait: live composite 25.0, rank 121, Developing. Bahrain: live composite 9.4, rank 166, Critical. The scan's 'never assessed' notes referred to the absence of a prior deep-dive report, not the absence of a published score. Both confirmed at their live baselines. DECISIVE PRINCIPLE: Iran's Jun 28 missile/drone attacks ON Kuwait and Bahrain are IRAN's conduct (scored against Iran), NOT the Gulf states' conduct. Each government was scored on its OWN conduct toward its own population: Kuwait intercepted two ballistic missiles with no fatalities (effective civilian protection) and ran no internal crackdown; Bahrain's civil defence worked the damaged Muharraq building with no casualties. Being attacked does not lower a compassion score. Surfaced on negative-theater events: §3e-bis(3) directionality blocks an upgrade (competent air defense is not strong countervailing compassion conduct); no new own-harm blocks a downgrade. Both CONFIRM at live baselines, delta 0.0.

false_positives_screened.screened: Iran's missile/drone strike is Iran's conduct, scored against Iran — not negative Kuwaiti own-conduct (attribution, per the task's explicit instruction). Kuwait's own conduct (effective interception, no fatalities, no internal crackdown) is competent civilian protection consistent with its Developing band; it is not strong countervailing compassion conduct that would clear an upgrade, and Kuwait surfaced on a negative-theater event (directionality blocks upgrade). No new own-harm blocks a downgrade. Baseline verified live (25.0/rank121), not from stale cache (20.0/rank126). Confirm.

Iranian drone harm in Muharraq is Iran's conduct (Amnesty's war-crimes research targets Iran), not Bahraini own-conduct (attribution). Bahrain's 9.4 already prices its OWN documented repression; no new within-window own-conduct either direction. If 9.4 is felt mis-calibrated vs Gulf peers, that is a coordinator-level calibration question, not an assessor one-off. Baseline verified live (9.4/rank166), not stale cache (4.4/rank170). Confirm.

Al Udeid strike and a Qatari shrapnel death are Iran's conduct (attribution). Qatar's Doha-mediation role is mild positive own-conduct but consistent with prior known behavior and sub-threshold; surfaced on a negative-theater event (no upgrade). No new negative own-conduct (no downgrade). Baseline verified live (10.9/rank163), not stale cache (5.9/rank165). Confirm.

Iranian strikes and Amnesty's war-crimes research are Iran's conduct (attribution), not Saudi's. Saudi's own chronic record (executions, dissent suppression) is already priced in 9.4; no new score-moving own-conduct in-window. Confirm.

The Gulf strikes ARE Iran's own negative conduct (correctly scored against Iran), but Iran is near the floor at 2.5 and the strikes targeted military bases with limited civilian casualties — a continuation of an already-priced aggression+execution pattern, not NEW mass deliberate harm clearing the 5-point threshold toward the floor. Standing-down for Doha talks is tactical, not positive compassion conduct (no upgrade). Confirm.

Jun 28 framework collapsed within 48 hours (Israel resumed strikes; Hezbollah rejection). Forward-trigger positive (implementation) did not materialize — no upgrade. Renewed war harm is Israel's conduct. No new Lebanese own-conduct; absence of new own-harm is not positive evidence.

The Jun 24 earthquakes are a natural disaster (exogenous, not own-conduct). Only the response is scorable and remains genuinely mixed: broad aid acceptance (mild positive; blocks downgrade) vs slow/opaque relief (negative; directionality blocks upgrade since surfaced on a negative event). Transparency concerns remain early allegations (pre-documentation). Net sub-threshold both directions. Confirm 18.0; watch ACT + ACC.
DOJ criminal/civil/antitrust probes are investigations, not convictions. Jun 23 takedown is sector context, not entity-named. Near-floor 10.2 already prices the alleged pattern.
35M Phase 3+ / 15,000 Phase 5 Borno already priced in the Jun 19 band crossing to 18.0. Re-scoring the same evidence would double-count. WFP cuts + Phase 5 declaration are forward triggers.

52.8M Sahel food insecurity + IDP totals continue the already-priced (Jun 1 reweighted) arc. The open Sahel-band calibration (Mali 12.5 vs BF 6.3) is a coordinator-level flag-for-review, not an assessor one-off.
M23 mass-killing harm attributed to Rwanda. DRC's own weak Ebola response reinforces the near-floor but continues already-priced compounding crises; no new threshold.

All at the absolute 0.0 floor. New negative own-conduct (Israel: COI genocide finding + resumed Lebanon strikes; Yemen: Houthi WFP staff terminations; Sudan: ceasefire rejection + El Obeid siege; South Sudan: deepening famine) reinforces but cannot lower a floor score. No positive own-conduct to support any upgrade.

math_hygiene.rotation_cache_status: Significant stale-cache drift found and corrected against authoritative live-index values (site/src/data/indexes/). Composite drift: Kuwait 20.0->25.0 (rank 126->121); Bahrain 4.4->9.4 (rank 170->166); Qatar 5.9->10.9 (rank 165->163). Rank-only drift refreshed: Lebanon 157->153, UnitedHealth 389->445, Israel 159->185, DRC 167->181, Saudi Arabia 158->169, Iran 178->180, Yemen 181->193, Sudan 184->190, South Sudan 186->189. Nigeria, Venezuela, Burkina Faso caches already matched live. Baselines read from live index, NOT the rotation cache. last_assessed bumped to 2026-06-29 for all 15.

sahel_calibration_status: OPEN — coordinator-level Sahel-band reweight review carried forward (Mali 12.5 vs Burkina Faso 6.3). Burkina Faso confirmed at 6.3; no assessor one-off (§3e-bis(5)).
attribution_note_openai: OpenAI did NOT appear in today's top-15 prioritized list and was not assessed. Recorded for completeness per the task's special-attention note: a US-government-mandated restricted release of GPT-5.6 would be US-government action affecting OpenAI (attribution to the US), not OpenAI's own compassion conduct.
forward_triggers_logged: Kuwait: documented internal crackdown or discriminatory denial of emergency protection (esp. bidoon/migrants) = downgrade; durable, verified expansion of bidoon/migrant rights = upgrade.
Bahrain: wartime crackdown/mass detention = downgrade; genuine release of political prisoners or restoration of civil/political rights = upgrade.
Qatar: wartime discrimination against migrants = downgrade; verified migrant-labor reform enforcement or a decisive attributable de-escalation role = upgrade.
Saudi Arabia: a documented new wave of mass executions or wartime crackdown = downgrade; verified durable reduction in executions = upgrade.
Iran: a documented new wave of mass executions or a deliberate mass-casualty strike on Gulf civilians = downgrade trigger toward the floor.
Lebanon: durable ceasefire + LAF sovereign control + reconstruction = positive own-conduct; expansion of internal armed conflict = negative own-conduct.
Sudan: an implemented, civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access = positive own-conduct.
Nigeria: realized WFP assistance cuts and a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno.
Venezuela: documented aid obstruction/diversion/casualty-data suppression (downgrade) or sustained effective transparent relief (upgrade).
UnitedHealth: a DOJ complaint, indictment, ruling, settlement, or admitted findings would be scorable.
